python3 << 'PY'
from PIL import Image
import os

src = '/tmp/ivx-logo-original.jpg'
img = Image.open(src).convert('RGB')

# Use high-quality upscaling to 1024x1024 (standard app icon size)
icon_size = (1024, 1024)
icon = img.resize(icon_size, Image.LANCZOS)

# Save as high-quality PNG
icon_path = 'assets/images/icon.png'
icon.save(icon_path, 'PNG', optimize=True)
print(f'Saved icon: {icon_path} -> {icon.size}')

# For adaptive icon, use the same full logo (black background blends with backgroundColor)
# The OS will mask it. Saving as PNG with high quality.
adaptive_path = 'assets/images/adaptive-icon.png'
icon.save(adaptive_path, 'PNG', optimize=True)
print(f'Saved adaptive icon: {adaptive_path}')

# Splash icon - same logo, slightly smaller for splash (also 1024x1024 is fine)
splash_path = 'assets/images/splash-icon.png'
icon.save(splash_path, 'PNG', optimize=True)
print(f'Saved splash icon: {splash_path}')

# Favicon 64x64
favicon = img.resize((64, 64), Image.LANCZOS)
favicon_path = 'assets/images/favicon.png'
favicon.save(favicon_path, 'PNG', optimize=True)
print(f'Saved favicon: {favicon_path}')

# Also save a copy for web/landing reuse
logo_path = 'assets/images/ivx-logo.png'
icon.save(logo_path, 'PNG', optimize=True)
print(f'Saved logo: {logo_path}')

for p in [icon_path, adaptive_path, splash_path, favicon_path, logo_path]:
    size = os.path.getsize(p)
    print(f'  {p}: {size} bytes')
PY

echo ""

python3 -c "
from PIL import Image
for p in ['icon.png', 'adaptive-icon.png', 'splash-icon.png', 'favicon.png']:
    img = Image.open(f'assets/images/{p}')
    print(f'{p}: {img.size}, mode: {img.mode}')
"
